# Broadside

> A Python framework for parallel LLM agent orchestration using scatter/gather instead of hierarchy.

Broadside implements the scatter/gather pattern as its core organizing principle. A task is scattered to N parallel agents (default 3-5), results are gathered, and a synthesis step produces a single output. No inter-agent communication, no hierarchy, no coordination overhead.

## Core concepts

- Task: a tightly scoped unit of work with a clear output
- Scatter: fan a task to N parallel agents
- Gather: collect and normalize outputs
- Synthesize: collapse N outputs into one (consensus, voting, or LLM-based)
- Budget: per-scatter token limits (scatter/gather multiplies costs ~15x)

## Backends

- Ollama (local, no API key, base install)
- Anthropic (optional extra)
- OpenAI-compatible (optional extra)

## Quick start

```python
import asyncio
from broadside import Task, run

task = Task(prompt="Your task here")
result = asyncio.run(run(task, n=3, backend="ollama"))
print(result.result)
```

## Links

- Source: https://github.com/jsesler/broadside
- Docs: https://jsesler.github.io/broadside
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/broadside/
